acceptance_criteria.description: TypeScript compiles with no errors after adding ring1.ts and ring2.ts
Ring 1 runner prepends shared system prompt, dispatches to per-level prompt templates, invokes claude-cli, and returns parsed Ring1Result
acceptance_criteria.rubric: Verify: (1) ring1.ts exports a function that accepts a rule ID, document content, and document level. (2) It dispatches to the correct per-level Ring 1 prompt template function based on document level (spec, impl, task). (3) The shared Ring 1 system prompt is defined as a constant and prepended to the per-level prompt. (4) It invokes the claude-cli wrapper with the combined prompt and the Ring1Result JSON schema. (5) The returned object conforms to Ring1Result (check, verdict, issues). PASS if all five hold; FAIL if any is missing.

acceptance_criteria.description: Ring 2 runner follows the same pattern as Ring 1 but with Ring 2 system prompt and Ring2Result schema
acceptance_criteria.rubric: Verify: (1) ring2.ts exports a function that accepts a rule ID, document content, and document level. (2) It dispatches to the correct per-level Ring 2 prompt template function based on document level. (3) The shared Ring 2 system prompt is defined as a constant and prepended to the per-level prompt. (4) It invokes the claude-cli wrapper with the combined prompt and the Ring2Result JSON schema. (5) The returned object conforms to Ring2Result (check, dimension, verdict, evidence, summary). PASS if all five hold; FAIL if any is missing.
